title: Executive Order 9639—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate Certain Plants and Facilities Used in the Transportation, Refining and Processing of Petroleum and Petroleum Products

flags.justification: Broad, open-ended authority ('in any manner that he deems necessary') applied across dozens of named companies spanning the entire national petroleum refining, transportation, and pipeline industry — the largest-scale application of this authority type in the corpus, though the same concentration serves rather than defeats the order's stated purpose.
Sec. 7 sets a concrete cap: termination 'in no event more than sixty days after' productive efficiency is restored, for each plant or facility individually.
Despite covering dozens of distinct companies, this is one coherent action addressing a single nationwide labor disruption in one industry.
Sec. 3 requires operation under 'terms and conditions of employment which are lawfully in effect at the time possession... is taken' — a real status-quo constraint.

Sec. 5 authorizes FBI investigation of 'any matter affecting the operation' of the seized facilities upon the Secretary's request, with no defined standard — the same open-ended concern found in EO 9595, now applicable across an entire national industry rather than one company.
The 60-day cap in Sec. 7 is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap and status-quo-employment-terms requirement provide real, checkable constraints on the core seizure authority; the FBI-investigation concern is captured under Flag 5.

This order applies the established seizure template at a dramatically larger scale than any prior instance in this corpus — an entire national industry seized simultaneously — a meaningful precedent for the scale at which this authority can be invoked in a single instrument.
Sec. 6's cooperation directive spans six federal agencies but is framed as support for the Secretary's exercise of authority, not a redirection of any of their own mandates.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 3/20 = 15.0%. The largest-scale seizure order in this corpus by far (dozens of companies, entire national petroleum industry), yet scores similarly to single-company seizures with comparable design features — scale alone does not drive structural weight, design does; the scale itself is credited only under Flag 9's second-order-effects finding.
